label: Localized topical antibiotic contact rash without infection or systemic reaction
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized topical antibiotic contact skin reaction.
No anaphylaxis, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal involvement, skin infection, severe medication reaction, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed plan.
exclusion_criteria: Anaphylaxis, airway symptoms, hypotension, systemic allergic reaction, mucosal lesions, severe cutaneous adverse reaction, cellulitis or wound infection concern, rapidly progressive rash, immunocompromised host, pregnancy/pediatric pathway, or specialist-directed dermatology/allergy/wound plan.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care, urgent care, dermatology, allergy, wound care, or the ED if symptoms are not improving or your clinician told you to recheck.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus supports contact dermatitis framing from irritant or allergic exposures, trigger avoidance, gentle skin care, clinician-directed topical or allergy treatment, and escalation for severe allergic, infectious, or mucosal red flags. This expansion is limited to clinician-diagnosed localized topical antibiotic contact skin reaction with anaphylaxis, systemic reaction, infection, mucosal, pregnancy/pediatric, severe-rash, and specialist-directed pathways absent.

terms: clinician diagnosis localized topical antibiotic contact rash
discharge diagnosis localized topical antibiotic contact rash
localized topical antibiotic contact rash diagnosed

localized topical antibiotic contact rash diagnosed
